mission.title: open-policy-agent-opa-: Rego error or warning for unused local variable
mission.description: Rego error or warning for unused local variable. This issue affects 5+ users.

steps.title: Identify open-policy-agent-opa- troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your open-policy-agent-opa- deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa-
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa- -n open-policy-agent-opa- --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review open-policy-agent-opa- configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant open-policy-agent-opa- configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa-
kubectl get configmap -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=open-policy-agent-opa-
```
As a policy author, I sometimes make mistakes that OPA might be able to detect by declaring a local variable that never gets used.  It could be valuable for OPA to provide a warning, error, linter that lets me know.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Rego error or warning for unused local variable
steps.description:
Apply the configuration change to resolve the issue:
```yaml
deny {
    values := split(input.request.path, ":")    
    port := to_number(values[1])
    input.user == "alice"
}
```

steps.title: Confirm Rego error or warning for unused local variable is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa- -n open-policy-agent-opa- --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n open-policy-agent-opa- --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: See the fix PR for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/open-policy-agent/opa/pull/4262

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with open-policy-agent-opa- installed or the issue environment reproducible.
